name: Bavaria (Germany)

colors.symbolism: from the Wittelsbach coat of arms
from the Wittelsbach coat of arms

description: A field of white and blue lozenges (diamonds) running diagonally; this is the Rautenflagge (lozenge flag), one of two official Bavarian state flags.
history: Bavaria has two official state flags: the white-and-blue horizontal-striped flag and the lozenge flag shown here, both adopted in their modern form on 14 December 1953. The lozengy pattern derives from the arms of the Counts of Bogen, inherited by the Wittelsbachs in 1242, who used it as the dynastic coat of arms throughout their long reign over Bavaria.

features.symbolism: The lozengy (diamond) pattern is derived from the heraldry of the House of Wittelsbach, which ruled Bavaria for over 700 years.
